decks.description: This mono-red Dragons deck leans harder into the tribal payoff engine of Lathliss, Dragon Queen, who creates a 5/5 flying Dragon token every time another nontoken Dragon enters — meaning Rapacious Dragon, Furnace Whelp, Dragon Hatchling, or Hellkite Punisher all generate a free extra body off Lathliss, and her static ability then pumps the whole squadron before an attack. Gadrak, the Crown-Scourge wants an artifact-heavy board (fed by Rapacious Dragon's Treasures and Gadrak's own death-triggered Treasure generation) before it can swing, tying together the deck's incidental artifact count. Dragonlord's Servant discounts the Dragon spells to help you deploy threats faster, while Draconic Roar and Sarkhan's Rage provide direct removal or reach, the latter punishing a Dragon-less board with self-damage as the tradeoff for its size. Lightning Shrieker again offers a hit-and-run threat that avoids being permanently answered. The overall plan is to flood the skies with Dragons and tokens, then swing for a huge alpha strike backed by Lathliss's pump.

decks.description: This mono-red spells deck is built around cheap burn and prowess/damage synergies that reward casting instants and sorceries. Lightning Visionary grows with prowess whenever you cast a noncreature spell, while Weaver of Lightning pings an opposing creature for 1 damage every time you cast an instant or sorcery, turning even a cheap cantrip into extra removal. Lightning Phoenix rewards you for dealing 3+ damage to an opponent in a turn by letting you pay {R} to return it from the graveyard, so burn spells like Lightning Bolt, Homing Lightning, or Lightning Axe aimed at the opponent's face double as recursion fuel. Living Lightning gives back an instant/sorcery from the graveyard when it dies, adding resilience to the burn plan. The deck's game plan is to chain cheap removal and direct damage spells together, using Weaver of Lightning and Lightning Visionary to turn spellslinging into steady damage and threats while occasionally reanimating Lightning Phoenix for extra value.

decks.description: This mono-red burn deck doubles down on the noncombat-damage-to-face plan, using Ball Lightning as a one-shot 6/1 haste trampler and Chain Lightning/Lightning Bolt as efficient direct damage to trigger Lightning Phoenix's graveyard return whenever 3+ damage lands on an opponent in a turn. Lightning Visionary's prowess and Weaver of Lightning's automatic ping on every instant/sorceries cast reward a spell-dense curve, turning cheap burn into extra reach and removal. Riddle of Lightning adds a scry-into-damage tool that scales off the top card's mana value, and Chain Lightning can even loop back onto the caster if they pay to copy it, punishing greedy responses. Living Lightning's death trigger recurs a burn spell for extra value, keeping the damage flowing even after early exchanges. The deck wants to race with cheap efficient burn, hit the opponent for 3+ whenever possible to keep recurring Lightning Phoenix, and finish with direct damage once the board is clear.
tips: Deal 3+ to recur Lightning Phoenix
Attack immediately with Ball Lightning, it dies end of turn
Chain spells to trigger Weaver of Lightning pings

Living Lightning rebuys burn spells on death

decks.description: This Rakdos-leaning (mono-red splashing black via hybrid mana) Minotaurs tribal deck is built around Sethron, Hurloon General, who creates a free 2/3 Minotaur token every time he or another nontoken Minotaur enters, meaning Borderland Minotaur, Minotaur Skullcleaver, or Warfire Javelineer all generate extra bodies alongside their own effects. Herald's Horn, set to Minotaur, discounts your creature spells and digs for more Minotaurs off the top of your library, smoothing draws toward the tribal payoffs. Warfire Javelineer's ETB damage scales with instants/sorceries in the graveyard, giving early burn spells like Soul Sear or Mugging late-game upside as removal fuel, and Sethron's activated ability can pump the whole Minotaur board with menace and haste for a surprise alpha strike. Flurry of Horns adds two more hasty 2/3 bodies straight onto the token plan. The deck's game plan is to flood the board with Minotaur tokens off Sethron, buff and evasion-enable them, and swing for a fast, wide finish.

decks.description: This mono-red-leaning (splashing black via hybrid mana) Minotaurs deck again centers on Sethron, Hurloon General generating free 2/3 Minotaur tokens off every nontoken Minotaur entering, further supported by Rageblood Shaman, which gives every other Minotaur +1/+1 and trample, turning Sethron's token stream into a wide army of hard-to-block bodies. Flurry of Horns doubles down on instant token generation, while Sethron's own pump ability grants menace and haste for surprise wide swings. Traitorous Greed lets you steal an opposing creature to attack with (untapped and hasty) while also ramping two mana of any color, and Unleash Fury can double the power of a key attacker, often a trampling Minotaur, for a huge burst of damage. Soul Sear and Sure Strike round out the removal and combat-trick suite. The deck wants to flood the board with Minotaurs via Sethron and Flurry of Horns, buff them with Rageblood Shaman's trample anthem, and close games with a wide, hard-to-block alpha strike, occasionally punctuated by stealing a blocker with Traitorous Greed.

decks.description: This mono-red deck builds around a sacrifice engine that turns dying creatures into direct damage. Chained Brute and Hobblefiend both want a steady stream of fodder to sacrifice — Chained Brute untaps itself for {1} plus a sacrifice, while Hobblefiend grows with +1/+1 counters the same way — and Havoc Jester and Spiteful Prankster both trigger off any sacrifice or death, turning every sac outlet into a burn spell to the face. Zurzoth, Chaos Rider generates a steady stream of 1/1 Devil tokens (each of which deals 1 damage when it dies) whenever an opponent draws extra cards off his attack trigger, feeding the sacrifice engine, while Pitchburn Devils and Dance with Devils' tokens all deal damage on death, making Collateral Damage and Heartfire (both of which require sacrificing a creature) efficient ways to combo a chump-blocker's death trigger into extra reach. Traitorous Greed lets you steal an opponent's creature, swing with it, then sac it to one of your many outlets for even more value.

decks.description: Like its sibling, this deck is a mono-red Devils sacrifice deck focused on converting dying creatures into pings. Havoc Jester fires off 1 damage whenever you sacrifice anything, and Spiteful Prankster does the same whenever any creature dies, so sac outlets like Chained Brute, Hobblefiend, and the two copies of Torch Fiend all double as extra reach even when they're just being used for their stated effect. Tibalt's Rager and Pitchburn Devils both deal damage when they die, meaning Collateral Damage and Heartfire (which require sacrificing a creature) can be pointed at a Devil that's already dying to squeeze out two damage triggers from one sacrifice. Zurzoth, Chaos Rider keeps the sacrifice fuel flowing by minting 1/1 Devil tokens whenever an opponent draws extra cards on your turn, and his attack trigger forces a symmetrical wheel that refills your hand with more fodder and burn.

decks.description: This build leans further into the sacrifice-for-damage theme with Brash Taunter as a centerpiece: its indestructibility lets it soak up combat damage and redirect it straight to an opponent, and it can also fight opposing creatures to convert their power into direct damage. The rest of the shell is the familiar sac engine — Chained Brute and Hobblefiend consume fodder to untap or grow, while Havoc Jester and Spiteful Prankster punish every sacrifice or death with a free ping. Lightning-Core Excavator and Pitchburn Devils both convert themselves into burn on death or activation, feeding Collateral Damage's sacrifice requirement for extra value, and Dance with Devils supplies two token bodies that themselves deal 1 damage when they die, giving you cheap, repeatable sac fodder. Act of Treason and Traitorous Greed both let you borrow an opponent's creature to attack and then feed it to your sacrifice outlets.

decks.description: This version of the sacrifice-Devils deck adds a go-wide payoff in Hellrider, which pings the defending player once for every attacking creature, making a swarm of expendable tokens and small Devils into direct reach on top of combat damage. The sacrifice core remains: Chained Brute and Hobblefiend consume fodder for value, Havoc Jester and Spiteful Prankster convert every sacrifice or death into a burn trigger, and Barrage of Expendables gives you a repeatable {R} sac outlet to feed all of them. Sin Prodder forces opponents into an awkward choice each upkeep — mill a card for damage equal to its mana value, or let you draw it — generating steady incidental damage or card advantage. Collateral Damage, Act of Treason, and Traitorous Greed all let you turn a stolen or expendable creature into a direct damage spell, and Lightning-Core Excavator is another late-game sac outlet that always deals 3 to any target.

decks.description: This is a classic go-wide Goblin tribal deck that floods the board with cheap tokens and uses removal-on-a-stick creatures to clear the way for lethal swings. Beetleback Chief, Goblin Instigator, and Muxus, Goblin Grandee (which can cheat multiple 5-mana-or-less Goblins straight onto the battlefield) all generate extra bodies, while Goblin Commando and Volley Veteran double as removal, with Volley Veteran's damage scaling directly with your Goblin count. Goblin Shortcutter clears blockers so your wide board can connect, and Outnumber scales its damage off your total creature count, making it a bigger and bigger removal spell the more tokens you've made. Makeshift Munitions turns any excess token into a repeatable damage source, letting you convert an overextended board into reach once combat is done.

decks.description: This Goblin tribal deck builds a wide board of tokens from Beetleback Chief, Goblin Instigator, and Muxus, Goblin Grandee, then closes games with team-wide pump spells. Burn Bright and Battle-Rattle Shaman both add power across your board right before combat, turning a swarm of 1/1 tokens into a lethal alpha strike, and Muxus itself grows by +1/+1 for every other Goblin you control when it attacks, so a wide board makes Muxus a finisher on its own. Goblin Commando and Goblin Shortcutter help clear the way by removing or disabling blockers, and Outnumber scales its damage with your creature count just like the rest of this token-heavy shell. Makeshift Munitions gives you a way to convert extra tokens into direct damage once the board state stalls.
tips: Pump before attacking with Burn Bright
Widen board first, then swing with Muxus
Use Shortcutter to open lanes
Battle-Rattle Shaman on your biggest attacker
Scale Outnumber off token count

decks.description: This Goblin tribal deck is built around lord effects and token generation to overwhelm with a wide, fast board. Goblin Chieftain gives every other Goblin +1/+1 and haste, letting freshly made tokens from Beetleback Chief and Goblin Instigator attack the turn they enter, while Goblin Commando and Volley Veteran act as pseudo-removal that also scales with your Goblin count. Goblin Goon is an unusual finisher that can't attack or block unless you outnumber the opponent in creatures, which this token-heavy shell is built to guarantee. Goblin Shortcutter clears the way for your team to connect, and Makeshift Munitions lets you convert extra tokens into reach once the board is set.
tips: Land Goblin Chieftain early for haste swarm
Keep board wide for Goblin Goon
Use Shortcutter before big swings
Scale Volley Veteran with token count
Convert extras with Makeshift Munitions

decks.description: This is the most explosive of the Goblin token decks, built around Krenko, Mob Boss, whose activated ability creates a token for every Goblin you already control, letting a wide board explode into a huge army in a single turn once he untaps. Goblin Rally and the Goblin Instigator/Boggart Brute package fill the board early to make Krenko's first activation as big as possible, while Goblin Lore refills your hand at the cost of some randomness. Burn Bright and Outnumber both scale off your board state, turning Krenko's tokens into either a lethal alpha strike or a board-clearing removal spell, and Goblin Shortcutter and Goblin Commando clear blockers so your swarm can get through. Makeshift Munitions is the deck's last line of reach, sacrificing spare tokens for direct damage.
tips: Protect Krenko at all costs
Widen board before activating Krenko
Burn Bright for lethal swing
Scale Outnumber with tokens
Refill with Goblin Lore

decks.description: This mono-red deck is a big-creature burn deck that cares about hitting power 4 or more to unlock bonus effects. Turret Ogre deals 2 damage to each opponent when it enters if you already control another creature with power 4+, and Furious Rise gives you a free card each end step under the same condition, rewarding you for curving into large threats like Bone Pit Brute and Flametongue Kavu (whose ETB deals 4 damage to a creature, doubling as removal). Unleash Fury doubles a creature's power for a huge alpha strike, and Fling lets you sacrifice that same pumped-up creature to send its power straight at a player's face as a surprise finisher. Volcanic Salvo becomes cheap late in the game since its cost reduction scales with your total board power, letting a deck full of big attackers cast a two-target 6-damage sweeper for very little mana.
tips: Double up with Unleash Fury then Fling
Keep a 4-power creature out for Furious Rise
Flametongue Kavu doubles as removal
Cast Volcanic Salvo late for cheap sweep
Turret Ogre punishes empty boards

decks.description: Another big-power-matters burn deck, this build again turns on bonus effects whenever you control a creature with power 4 or greater. Turret Ogre and Flames of the Raze-Boar both check for that condition to deal extra damage — Turret Ogre pings each opponent on entry, while Flames of the Raze-Boar upgrades from single-target removal into a mini board wipe against that opponent's other creatures. Furious Rise keeps rewarding you with free cards off the same power-4 condition, so creatures like Bone Pit Brute and Borderland Marauder (which pumps itself when attacking) are doing double duty as both attackers and enablers. Fling remains the deck's reach tool, sacrificing a beefed-up creature for direct damage, and Hungry Flames splits removal and burn between a creature and a player in one card, closing out games once your big threats have done their job.
tips: Keep a 4-power body in play
Flames of the Raze-Boar as mini wipe
Fling big creatures for reach
Hungry Flames splits removal and burn
Equip Short Sword to push power

decks.description: This is a mono-red big-power aggro deck built around creatures with power 4 or greater triggering payoffs. Turret Ogre deals 2 damage to each opponent on entry if you already control a power-4+ creature, Sarkhan's Unsealing blasts opponents whenever you cast a big creature spell, and Furious Rise gives card advantage every turn a heavy hitter is on board. Hamletback Goliath snowballs by absorbing the power of every creature that enters after it as +1/+1 counters, turning subsequent Ogres and Brutes into huge threats. The deck also carries a sacrifice-damage subtheme: Fling and Heartfire Immolator's own sac ability let you convert a big attacker directly into reach damage, while Unleash Fury doubling a creature's power turns any attacker (or a Flung creature) into a game-ending burst. Bone Pit Brute's menace and pump, plus Inferno Hellion's trample and self-shuffle, keep pressure high while refilling your deck with threats.
tips: Hold Fling for lethal burst
Play power-4 threats before Turret Ogre
Feed Hamletback Goliath with sequencing
Cast big creatures to trigger Sarkhan's Unsealing
Use Unleash Fury on unblocked attacker

decks.description: A mono-red midrange-aggro deck centered on power-4-plus creatures and direct damage. Turret Ogre and Sarkhan's Unsealing (implicitly, though only Furious Rise and Turret Ogre appear here) reward you for curving into big bodies, while Furor of the Bitten cheaply turns any early creature into a 4/4-equivalent threat that must attack, keeping pressure constant. Etali, Primal Storm is the standout card advantage/damage engine: every time it connects in combat you exile the top card of each library and can cast them for free, potentially chaining extra spells and creatures in a single attack. Bloodshot Trainee doubles as reach removal once its power hits 4, and Fling plus Heartfire Immolator's sacrifice ability give you ways to convert a big attacker into direct damage to close out games or snipe blockers.
tips: Prioritize connecting with Etali
Enchant early creature with Furor of the Bitten
Pump Bloodshot Trainee to activate its ability
Fling a big creature for reach
Keep board with power 4+ for Turret Ogre/Furious Rise

decks.description: A red spells-matter deck that wants to chain cheap instants and sorceries to power up a suite of prowess and spell-triggered creatures. Thermo-Alchemist pings opponents for 1 every time you cast an instant or sorcery and untaps to do it again on your next spell, while Kinetic Augur grows to match your graveyard's instant/sorcery count and refills your hand on entry via loot. Double Vision copies the first spell you cast each turn, meaning a burn spell like Shock or Hungry Flames doubles up for extra damage, and can even copy Blindblast to draw two cards and stop two blockers. Chandra's Pyreling escalates into double strike whenever any of these noncombat damage sources connects, turning a small burn deck into a lethal finisher once it's online.
tips: Sequence spells to untap Thermo-Alchemist
Cast first spell before Double Vision copy
Fill graveyard for Kinetic Augur
Land noncombat damage to buff Chandra's Pyreling
Loot with Kinetic Augur to find burn

decks.description: This mono-red instants-and-sorceries deck leans hard into the classic 'spells matter' payoff creatures. Kiln Fiend swings for huge damage the turn you chain multiple cheap spells since it gets +3/+0 per instant/sorcery cast, while Young Pyromancer builds a token army off the same spells. Thermo-Alchemist provides a repeatable pinging engine that untaps whenever you cast another instant or sorcery, letting a single turn of burn spells net multiple triggers. Double Vision doubles your first spell each turn, so casting Flame Lash or Hungry Flames with Double Vision out effectively deals double damage from one card, and Kinetic Augur grows with your graveyard while looting away excess lands. The deck's plan is to empty its hand of burn in a single big turn to combine Kiln Fiend's damage, Young Pyromancer's tokens, and Thermo-Alchemist pings into a fast kill.
tips: Chain spells to pump Kiln Fiend
Cast burn before Double Vision resolves
Untap Thermo-Alchemist each spell
Use Young Pyromancer tokens as blockers/attackers
Loot with Kinetic Augur for gas

decks.description: This build doubles down on graveyard recursion of instants and sorceries. Charmbreaker Devils returns a random instant/sorcery from the yard each upkeep and grows +4/+0 whenever you cast one, Living Lightning brings back a spell when it dies, and Kinetic Augur both scales off graveyard spell count and loots on entry, creating a loop of recurring the same burn spells. Dualcaster Mage is the deck's centerpiece combo piece: it flashes in and copies any instant or sorcery you or an opponent casts, letting you get two-for-one value off a Shock, Hungry Flames, or even an opponent's removal spell. Thermo-Alchemist keeps chipping away with pings that untap on every spell cast, and Chandra's Pyreling threatens to close games out with double strike once noncombat damage starts flowing.
tips: Flash Dualcaster Mage to copy key spells
Recur burn with Charmbreaker Devils
Return spells via Living Lightning death trigger
Keep casting instants to untap Thermo-Alchemist
Copy opponent's removal with Dualcaster Mage

decks.description: A tight mono-red burn/spells deck that wants to maximize damage from each instant or sorcery cast. Kiln Fiend and Kinetic Augur both scale with your spellslinging (temporary +3/+0 per spell for Kiln Fiend, permanent power equal to graveyard instants/sorceries for Augur), while Doublecast lets you copy any burn spell you cast that turn for extra reach, and Crash Through both draws a card and grants trample to push through blockers. Immolating Gyre acts as a graveyard-scaling board wipe against small creatures, dealing damage equal to your instant/sorcery count in the yard. Thermo-Alchemist and Chandra's Pyreling round out the damage-engine plan, pinging opponents and eventually gaining double strike once enough noncombat damage has been dealt, letting the deck close out games through repeated burn triggers rather than combat alone.
tips: Copy key burn with Doublecast
Grow Kinetic Augur via graveyard spells
Trample through with Crash Through
Wipe small boards with Immolating Gyre
Chain spells to pump Kiln Fiend

decks.description: A green midrange deck focused on big creatures (power 4+) unlocking a suite of payoffs. Drowsing Tyrannodon can only attack once you control a power-4+ creature, and Garruk's Uprising both draws a card on entry if that condition is met and keeps drawing every time a big creature enters, while also handing your whole team trample. Garruk, Unleashed pumps a creature for a huge trample swing or builds an army of 3/3 Beast tokens, and its ultimate can put creatures directly into play for free. Hunter's Insight turns a single unblocked hit into a card-draw haul equal to the damage dealt, making Garruk's own +3/+3 pump or Affectionate Indrik's fight ability (which can also just clear a blocker) especially valuable. Garruk's Harbinger further compounds the plan by digging for more creatures or Garruk copies whenever it connects, so the whole deck is geared toward getting one big creature through combat to snowball card advantage.
tips: Land a power-4+ creature early
Draw off Garruk's Uprising triggers
Combo Hunter's Insight with a pumped attacker
Fight down blockers with Affectionate Indrik
Use Garruk's +1 for trample burst damage

decks.description: This green deck flips the defender archetype on its head, using a wall of high-toughness blockers to stall while Assault Formation and Warmonger's Chariot turn them into attackers. Assault Formation makes every creature assign combat damage equal to toughness instead of power, meaning even a 0/4 Wall of Vines suddenly hits like a truck, and its {2}{G} ability further inflates everyone's toughness for even bigger damage. Warmonger's Chariot and Roving Keep's own {7} ability let a defender push through despite its 'can't attack' text, while Overgrown Battlement taps for a mountain of mana equal to your defender count, ramping into Towering Titan, which enters with +1/+1 counters equal to the combined toughness of your other creatures for an enormous body. Card draw from Carven Caryatid, Wall of Blossoms, and Portcullis Vine (which can sacrifice a defender to draw) keeps the hand full while you wait out the opponent behind an unbreakable wall.
tips: Ramp with Overgrown Battlement
Turn on attacks via Assault Formation
Equip Warmonger's Chariot to swing walls
Grow Towering Titan off toughness
Draw cards from Wall ETBs

decks.description: A green Cat tribal deck that wants to swing wide and get creatures through combat damage for value. Feline Sovereign is the anchor, buffing every other Cat by +1/+1, granting protection from Dogs, and destroying an artifact or enchantment whenever your Cats connect, while Keeper of Fables draws a card whenever any non-Human creature (which includes most of your Cats) deals combat damage. Pridemalkin's +1/+1 counter grants trample to any creature that has one, synergizing directly with Sabertooth Mauler's ability to add a counter and untap itself whenever a creature died that turn, letting it become an ever-growing trampling blocker/attacker. Canopy Stalker forces blocks and gains life off any creature dying that turn, which combines with Sabertooth Mauler and Feral Prowler's card-draw-on-death to turn combat trades into resource advantage. Enlarge is the deck's finisher, giving +7/+7, trample, and a forced block to close out a game in one swing.
tips: Buff team with Feline Sovereign
Force blocks with Canopy Stalker
Grow Sabertooth Mauler off deaths
Trample via Pridemalkin counters
Finish with Enlarge on unblocked attacker

decks.description: This green Cat tribal build leans on combat-damage triggers and death-value creatures to grind out card advantage while building a board. Feline Sovereign pumps every other Cat and punishes opponents' artifacts/enchantments whenever Cats connect, while Keeper of Fables draws cards off any non-Human combat damage, rewarding wide attacks. Penumbra Bobcat and Feral Prowler both convert removal or combat losses into value, leaving behind a token or a card draw when they die, and Lurking Predators turns your opponent's own spellcasting against them by potentially dropping free creatures onto the battlefield off the top of your library every time they cast a spell. Pridemalkin's +1/+1 counter grants trample to any counter-bearing creature, and Enlarge or Nature's Way can push a key attacker through combat or clear a problematic blocker to keep the aggression going.
tips: Attack wide to trigger Keeper of Fables
Let Lurking Predators cheat creatures in
Trade Penumbra Bobcat for free token
Trample with Pridemalkin counters
Fight blockers with Nature's Way

decks.description: This is a mono-green tribal Elves deck that grinds out incremental value with mana-dorks and card selection before closing the game with a single explosive overrun turn. Elvish Archdruid pumps every other Elf +1/+1 and taps for a huge burst of green mana off your Elf count, letting you deploy threats far ahead of curve; Llanowar Visionary and Silhana Wayfinder smooth draws while Dwynen's Elite and Presence of Gond generate extra Elf Warrior tokens to widen the board. The whole plan is building toward Craterhoof Behemoth, whose enter-the-battlefield trigger gives every creature you control trample and +X/+X equal to your creature count — after a game of tokens and Elf swarming, this single card can end the game out of nowhere. Along the way, Hunter's Edge and Titanic Growth serve as pseudo-removal/combat tricks that use the counters and pump theme to trade up or push through extra damage.
tips: Flood board with Elf tokens
Tap Archdruid for burst mana
Save Craterhoof for lethal swing
Use Hunter's Edge as removal
Protect key mana dorks

decks.description: A leaner, more midrange-y Elves build than its counterpart, this deck leans on Allosaurus Shepherd to protect its spells from countermagic (both itself and other green spells) while its late-game ability can turn every Elf into a uniform 5/5 Dinosaur beater, instantly transforming a board of small mana dorks and tokens into a wall of large attackers. Dwynen's Elite and Presence of Gond still generate Elf Warrior tokens to go wide, and Silhana Wayfinder plus Llanowar Visionary smooth the draw, but without Craterhoof Behemoth this version wins more through grinding combat math, deathtouch trades from Wren's Run Vanquisher, and eventually flipping the whole team into 5/5s with Allosaurus Shepherd's activated ability once you have the mana to spare.
tips: Cast key spells behind Shepherd
Build wide with Elf tokens
Save mana for 5/5 flip
Trade up with deathtouch blocker
Don't overextend into removal early

decks.description: This deck is a landfall/lands-matter ramp deck that treats its mana base as a second army. Zendikar's Roil turns every land drop into a free 2/2 Elemental token, while Vastwood Zendikar (Vastwood Zendikon) and Elemental Uprising can animate lands themselves into 6/4 or 4/4 threats, meaning even a flooded hand still produces bodies. Sylvan Ranger, Cultivate, and Fertilid all ramp and fix mana, feeding Ulvenwald Hydra (whose power/toughness equals your land count and which also fetches a land on entry) and Awakener Druid, which turns a Forest into a 4/5 Treefolk creature that survives as long as Awakener Druid does. The synergy is explicit: every ramp spell that puts a land onto the battlefield also triggers Zendikar's Roil for another token, so the deck can flood the board with Elemental tokens purely from playing its normal ramp package.
tips: Play extra lands for landfall
Ramp feeds Zendikar's Roil tokens
Animate lands to dodge sweepers
Grow Ulvenwald Hydra with more lands
Protect Awakener Druid to keep Treefolk

decks.description: Similar to its companion deck, this build doubles down on landfall payoffs and extra land drops. Oracle of Mul Daya lets you play an extra land each turn and cast lands straight off the top of your library, dramatically accelerating how often Sporemound's landfall trigger fires to make 1/1 Saproling tokens. Fertilid, Sylvan Ranger, and Cultivate all ramp additional lands into play, each one triggering Sporemound again, while Woodborn Behemoth rewards reaching eight-plus lands with a huge +4/+4 trample buff and Feral Hydra can be grown indefinitely by any player paying {3}. The deck's core interaction is straightforward: Oracle of Mul Daya plus any land-ramp spell means multiple land drops and Sporemound triggers in a single turn, quickly overwhelming opponents with disposable tokens while your ramp outpaces them on lands.
tips: Chain ramp spells for tokens
Play lands off Oracle's top card
Push toward 8 lands for Behemoth
Grow Feral Hydra opportunistically
Use Awakener Druid on threatened Forests

decks.description: This deck plays a big-creature aggro game built around cheating out and enabling giant Dinosaurs. Ghalta, Primal Hunger becomes drastically cheaper the more total power you already have on board, so early plays like Colossal Dreadmaw and Ornery Dilophosaur (which gets +2/+2 when attacking alongside a power-4+ creature) set up an early, discounted Ghalta. Drowsing Tyrannodon sits as a defender until you have a power-4+ creature, at which point it can attack freely, and Setessan Training replaces itself with a card while giving a creature trample. Savage Stomp is discounted to fight off blockers when targeting a Dinosaur, giving the deck cheap removal that also grows a threat, and Commune with Dinosaurs digs for more Dinosaurs or lands to keep the curve moving.
tips: Curve into cheap Ghalta
Attack with power-4 creatures first
Use Savage Stomp as removal
Unlock Drowsing Tyrannodon with big threats
Dig with Commune for Dinosaurs

decks.description: This version of the Dinosaur deck emphasizes card advantage and mana acceleration on top of the usual big-creature beatdown plan. Selvala, Heart of the Wilds lets opponents' or your own huge creatures draw cards on entry if they have the highest power on the battlefield, and can tap for enormous mana equal to your biggest creature's power, letting you cast expensive threats early. Garruk's Uprising is the standout payoff: it gives every creature you control trample and draws a card every time a power-4-or-greater creature enters, meaning Colossal Dreadmaw, Thundering Spineback's token generation, or any big Dinosaur hitting the battlefield refuels your hand while making your whole team hit harder. New Horizons ramps mana while also placing a +1/+1 counter on a creature, tightening the mana curve for your bigger spells.
tips: Play Garruk's Uprising early
Chain big Dinosaurs for draws
Tap Selvala off biggest threat
Use Savage Stomp to remove blockers
Ramp with New Horizons for early plays

decks.description: A straightforward big-Dinosaur aggro deck that combines cost-reduction, tribal lords, and combat tricks to push through large amounts of damage. Ghalta, Primal Hunger again gets cheaper based on your total board power, so early plays from Colossal Dreadmaw and Drover of the Mighty (which gets +2/+2 while you control a Dinosaur) set up a fast, discounted Ghalta later. Thundering Spineback buffs every other Dinosaur and can pump out 3/3 trample tokens in the late game, while Savage Stomp doubles as cheap removal and a counter-growth spell when aimed at a Dinosaur. Titanic Growth and Commune with Dinosaurs round things out as a combat trick and card selection to keep the curve flowing toward your biggest threats.
tips: Build power before casting Ghalta
Buff team with Spineback
Use Savage Stomp for removal
Save Titanic Growth for lethal
Dig with Commune for threats

decks.description: This Dinosaur build leans hardest into the card-draw engine of Garruk's Uprising, using it to refill your hand every time a big creature enters while also granting the whole team trample. Rampaging Brontodon scales with your land count on attack, becoming a massive trampler in the late game, and Primal Might doubles as both a pump spell and a fight-based removal spell that can grow a creature past 4 power to trigger Garruk's Uprising's draw before combat even starts. Savage Stomp again offers discounted removal on Dinosaurs, and New Horizons accelerates your mana while adding a +1/+1 counter, helping you hit your big Dinosaur drops on time and keep chaining Garruk's Uprising triggers.
tips: Land Garruk's Uprising ASAP
Pump with Primal Might pre-combat
Grow Brontodon with more lands
Use Savage Stomp as removal
Ramp via New Horizons

decks.description: This is a dedicated +1/+1 counters synergy deck where nearly every card either places counters or benefits from them. Wildwood Scourge enters with X counters and then grows further whenever any other non-Hydra creature you control gets a counter, so Ironshell Beetle, Pridemalkin, or Trufflesnout entering and dropping a counter on something else still triggers Wildwood Scourge's own growth. Pridemalkin grants trample to every creature with a counter, turning your whole counter-laden board into evasive attackers, while Armorcraft Judge cashes in the counter theme for a burst of card draw equal to how many creatures you control have a counter. Primeval Bounty is the deck's value engine, creating 3/3 Beast tokens off creature spells, dropping three counters on a creature off noncreature spells, and gaining life on every land drop, giving multiple ways to keep generating counters and bodies each turn. Invigorating Surge is the standout combo piece: doubling the counters on a creature that already has several counters (especially Wildwood Scourge) can produce a massive one-turn swing.
tips: Grow Wildwood Scourge passively
Double up with Invigorating Surge
Draw cards off Armorcraft Judge
Attack wide with Pridemalkin trample
Cast spells to trigger Primeval Bounty

decks.description: A tighter, more explosive version of the counters theme, this deck is built to make Wildwood Scourge and Branching Evolution snowball out of control. Branching Evolution doubles every +1/+1 counter placed on your creatures, so any counter-adding effect — Ironshell Beetle's or Pridemalkin's enter-the-battlefield counter, or Invigorating Surge's counter-then-double effect — becomes twice as impactful, and stacking Invigorating Surge with Branching Evolution can quadruple a single counter placement. Wildwood Scourge still grows whenever another non-Hydra creature gets a counter, meaning Branching Evolution's doubling effect also doubles Wildwood Scourge's own trigger count in a given instance, rapidly inflating it into a massive threat. Pridemalkin's trample-granting static ability ensures that once creatures start piling up +1/+1 counters, they can actually push damage through instead of getting chump blocked, and Armorcraft Judge again converts a wide board of counter-creatures into a big card draw payoff.
tips: Land Branching Evolution early
Stack Invigorating Surge for doubling
Grow Wildwood Scourge fast
Push damage with Pridemalkin trample
Cash in counters via Armorcraft Judge

decks.description: This mono-green deck builds a board of counter-laden creatures and then leverages payoffs that scale off how many counters are in play. Fertilid and Scrounging Bandar enter with two +1/+1 counters, Rishkar, Peema Renegade and Pridemalkin spread counters onto other creatures, and Wildwood Scourge grows every time another non-Hydra creature gets a counter, turning it into a snowballing threat as your other creatures are buffed. Rishkar's ability also turns every counter-bearing creature into a green mana source, letting you ramp into Invigorating Surge, which doubles a creature's counters outright for explosive growth, or into Hunter's Edge and Arbor Armament to remove blockers and push counters further. The clincher is Inspiring Call, which draws a card for every creature you control with a counter while also making them all indestructible, letting you refill your hand and survive a board wipe or combat trick blowout in one shot. Trufflesnout and Nessian Hornbeetle give flexible curve filling, either adding a counter or stabilizing life, while Pridemalkin's static ability grants trample to any creature with a counter, letting your fatties actually push through damage.
tips: Spread counters early with Pridemalkin/Rishkar
Rishkar turns counter creatures into mana
Save Invigorating Surge for a big counter creature
Inspiring Call refills hand and protects board
Wildwood Scourge grows off every other counter

decks.description: A tighter, more explosive version of the counters theme built around doubling effects. Branching Evolution doubles every +1/+1 counter placed on your creatures, so Pridemalkin's entry trigger, Scrounging Bandar's built-in counters, and Lifecrafter's Gift's counter-spreading all become twice as effective, and stacking it with Invigorating Surge (which independently doubles a single creature's counters) can produce absurd single-creature growth in one turn. Champion of Lambholt punishes opponents for going wide since it grows every time you play another creature and shuts down blocks from anything weaker, while Wildwood Scourge again scales off every counter placed on your other non-Hydra creatures, benefiting hugely from Branching Evolution's doubling. Hunter's Edge doubles as both removal and a counter-placement trigger, letting a buffed creature fight down a blocker while still growing your board. This deck wants to jam a couple of key counter-generators onto the battlefield, then use Branching Evolution and Invigorating Surge to snowball them into lethal threats that Pridemalkin's trample-granting ability lets punch through for the win.
tips: Land Branching Evolution before counter spells
Stack doublers with Invigorating Surge
Champion of Lambholt punishes blockers
Hunter's Edge doubles as removal
Pridemalkin grants trample to push damage

decks.description: A Golgari-adjacent mono-green (splashing red via Neyith) fight/aggro deck that wants to force combat and profit from creatures dying. Somberwald Stag and Time to Feed let you fight down opposing blockers before combat even starts, and Irresistible Prey forces a blocker into a fight it can't avoid while replacing itself with a card. Sabertooth Mauler snowballs off any creature dying that turn, growing and untapping to become a repeated blocker or attacker, while Brindle Shoat trades up into a 3/3 Boar token when it dies, giving you resilient value even when it's removed or sacrificed. Neyith of the Dire Hunt ties the whole plan together: it draws a card whenever your creatures fight or become blocked, and its combat ability can double a creature's power while forcing it to be blocked, guaranteeing both a kill and a card draw off Neyith's first trigger. Fungal Rebirth further rewards a creature dying that turn by returning a permanent from the graveyard while also making two Saproling tokens, so the deck wants combat and removal trades happening every turn to keep the value engine running.
tips: Force blocks with Irresistible Prey
Neyith doubles power and draws cards
Sabertooth Mauler thrives on death triggers
Fungal Rebirth needs a creature to die
Trade up with Brindle Shoat's death token

decks.description: This version of the fight-deck leans further into forcing unfavorable combats while adding evasion and a small life-gain safety net. Affectionate Indrik gives you another on-demand fight to clear a blocker on entry, complementing Time to Feed and Irresistible Prey, and Neyith of the Dire Hunt continues to draw cards off fights and forced blocks while its combat ability can double a creature's power and make it unblockable-in-reverse by forcing a block. Gingerbrute adds a hasty, cheaply-unblockable attacker that can also be sacrificed later for 3 life if the game goes long, giving the deck a way to close games or pivot to defense. Sabertooth Mauler again rewards any creature dying that turn with a permanent +1/+1 counter and an untap, so combining it with Time to Feed's or Irresistible Prey's forced combats lets it grow every single turn. Fungal Rebirth rebuys a key permanent from the graveyard and mints two Saprolings whenever a creature died that turn, meaning this deck wants to keep trading creatures in combat to fuel both its card advantage and its board presence.
tips: Fight down blockers with Indrik/Stag effects
Gingerbrute pushes early unblockable damage
Neyith rewards forced blocks with cards
Keep Sabertooth Mauler fed with deaths
Fungal Rebirth rebuys key permanents

decks.description: A grindy green fight deck that trades combat aggression for life-total security. Thragtusk is the centerpiece: it gains you 5 life on entry and leaves behind a 3/3 Beast token when it dies or leaves play, meaning even removal or a sacrifice effect nets you a body and a life swing. Somberwald Stag clears a blocker on entry via fight, and Time to Feed combines a forced fight with 3 life gain if the targeted creature dies, stacking further with Brindle Shoat's death trigger that leaves a 3/3 Boar behind. Sabertooth Mauler grows and untaps whenever a creature died that turn, so this deck's steady stream of fight-based removal (Time to Feed, Somberwald Stag) keeps feeding it bigger and more active. Gingerbrute offers an early evasive clock or a late-game 3-life sac outlet, and Fungal Rebirth's two-Saproling bonus triggers reliably given how many death effects the deck runs, giving this list a slower, attrition-based game plan focused on outlasting opponents rather than racing them.
tips: Thragtusk nets life and a Beast token
Time to Feed doubles as fight and lifegain
Feed Sabertooth Mauler with combat trades
Fungal Rebirth rewards deaths with Saprolings
Grind opponents out, don't rush

decks.description: This build turns the fight/death subtheme into a card-draw engine anchored by Momentous Fall, which lets you sacrifice a creature to draw cards equal to its power and gain life equal to its toughness. Ravenous Baloth's sacrifice ability only needs a Beast to fuel free life gain, and creatures like Affectionate Indrik (fight on entry) and Time to Feed (forced fight with lifegain) soften up blockers before you commit to bigger swings. Sabertooth Mauler again grows and untaps whenever anything dies that turn, meaning every fight from Affectionate Indrik or Time to Feed also fuels the Mauler passively. The deck's real payoff turn is sacrificing a big, already-value creature like Trufflesnout or Sylvan Brushstrider to Momentous Fall after they've already banked life gain or counters, turning a creature that's done its job into a burst of cards and life. Brindle Shoat's death trigger and Fungal Rebirth's Saproling bonus give extra bodies and graveyard recursion to keep feeding the sacrifice/fight shell.
tips: Sac big creatures to Momentous Fall
Use Affectionate Indrik/Time to Feed as removal

decks.description: A card-draw-centric green deck where nearly every creature and spell replaces itself, building toward big turns where Burlfist Oak and Gnarled Sage explode in size. Llanowar Visionary and Wall of Blossoms draw a card on entry while also ramping or blocking, and Primordial Sage turns every subsequent creature you cast into an optional draw, compounding your card advantage fast. Burlfist Oak gets +2/+2 until end of turn for every single card you draw, so casting Aggressive Urge or Explore (both cantrips) after Burlfist Oak is in play can massively pump it in the same turn, and Gnarled Sage similarly grows tougher and gains vigilance once you've drawn two-plus cards that turn. Warden of the Woods punishes opponents who try to interact with it by drawing you two cards whenever it's targeted, adding a layer of removal-deterrence to the value engine. Nature's Way lets a big Burlfist Oak fight down a blocker with vigilance and trample, letting the deck close games with the same creature it just used its card draw to inflate.

decks.description: This deck follows the same card-draw shell but leans on Wildsize instead of Aggressive Urge for its combat trick, giving a bigger one-time pump alongside its cantrip. Llanowar Visionary and Wall of Blossoms draw a card immediately on entry, feeding Burlfist Oak's whenever-you-draw +2/+2 trigger, so playing either of them and then following up with Wildsize or Explore in the same turn can turn Burlfist Oak into a genuine threat out of nowhere. Primordial Sage keeps the card advantage flowing by turning every creature you cast into a potential draw, and Gnarled Sage becomes a sturdy 0/4-ish reach blocker with vigilance once you've drawn multiple cards in a turn. Warden of the Woods again punishes any removal or bounce aimed at it by drawing you two cards, discouraging opponents from interacting with your board. Nature's Way remains the deck's finishing tool, letting a pumped creature fight through a blocker with trample and vigilance so it can attack and still hold the line on defense.

decks.description: This build adds Jolrael, Mwonvuli Recluse to the card-draw shell, turning your natural stream of cantrips into a token engine: since Llanowar Visionary, Wall of Blossoms, Aggressive Urge, and Explore all draw cards, drawing a second card in a turn off any of these triggers Jolrael's ability to create a free 2/2 Cat, and later in the game Jolrael's activated ability can turn your whole board into X/X creatures based on cards in hand. Burlfist Oak continues to punish every draw with a temporary +2/+2, meaning a turn where you play Llanowar Visionary (draw one) then cast Aggressive Urge (draw two) both grows Burlfist Oak twice and creates a Cat token off Jolrael simultaneously. Gnarled Sage and Warden of the Woods round out the defensive shell, growing tougher after multiple draws and punishing removal aimed at them respectively. Nature's Way remains the go-to way to convert a pumped creature into removal plus a combat swing, letting this grindy value deck eventually overwhelm opponents with card advantage, extra bodies, and oversized threats.

decks.description: This version swaps in Soul of the Harvest and Verdant Embrace to push the card-draw plan toward a stickier, more resilient endgame. Soul of the Harvest draws a card whenever another nontoken creature enters, stacking with Llanowar Visionary and Wall of Blossoms' own entry draws to flood your hand, while Burlfist Oak turns each of those draws into a temporary +2/+2, letting it become a surprise attacker or blocker on a turn where you chain several creatures or cantrips. Verdant Embrace turns any creature into a permanent token generator, granting +3/+3 and creating a 1/1 Saproling every upkeep, which pairs well with Soul of the Harvest since each new Saproling that enters (as long as it isn't itself a token benefiting) keeps feeding your board while Wildsize and Explore keep the draw triggers coming. Warden of the Woods again punishes removal by drawing two cards when targeted, and Gnarled Sage becomes a solid reach blocker once you've drawn multiple cards in a turn. Overall this is a grindy, resilient value deck that wants to bury opponents in card advantage and steadily accumulating token/creature presence.

decks.description: This deck is a five-color goodstuff pile built around heavy mana fixing that lets its splashy multicolor payoffs come online as early as possible. Alloy Myr, Prismite, Prophetic Prism, Skittering Surveyor, Mirrodin's Core, Rupture Spire, and Terramorphic Expanse all exist purely to smooth draws and enable casting cards regardless of color, which is critical since the deck's best cards want mana of every color in play. Chamber Sentry rewards exactly this plan: it enters with a +1/+1 counter for every distinct color of mana spent on it, so with all five fixers active it can come down as a sizable threat that can later tap and remove counters to shoot for damage, and if it dies it can even be returned to hand by paying WUBRG. Ironroot Warlord scales with your board's creature count for a cheap, ever-growing attacker, while Maelstrom Archangel turns combat damage into a free extra spell from hand, letting you double-spend mana once she connects. Auger Spree and Lawmage's Binding provide flexible removal (either a huge power/toughness swing to kill something or a hard lock that shuts down attacks, blocks, and activated abilities), and Dinrova Horror combines a tempo bounce with a discard hit for extra disruption. Overall this is a midrange value deck that leans on fixing to consistently deploy its color-hungry bombs rather than any single tight combo.
tips: Fix mana before big plays
